remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet?
remarks.content: In short: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA released OpenShell, an open source runtime that sandboxes autonomous AI agents with kernel-level policy: what files they can touch, what processes they can spawn, where their traffic can go.... The post NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet? appeared
remarks.contentTranslation: 简而言之：在GTC 2026上，NVIDIA发布了OpenShell，这是一个开源运行时，通过内核级策略对自主AI代理进行沙盒化隔离：它们可以访问哪些文件、可以生成哪些进程、流量可以流向何处……《NVIDIA OpenShell 守护代理安全，谁来管理整个集群？》一文随之发布。
remarks.translation: NVIDIA OpenShell 保障智能体安全。谁来管理整个智能体集群？
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
What I just dictated into ChatGPT Work

> Okay, so I received a lot of DMs for, I did a call out for ChatGPT Work, basically early access beta group. I want you to go through all my DMs on Twitter and then just take all of them that mention ChatGPT Work. It's like a lot of them, thousands, and put them all in a spreadsheet. What I want in the spreadsheet is the name, I want the link to the the DM, so I can go back to it quickly. I want the post that they did, but remove any new lines, so it's just in a single cell. And then I want a column which is basically your assessment of what they're doing, right? So for example, if it's like sales, put sales, if it's marketing, put marketing, if it's design, put design. Come up with your own taxonomy for how to classify their work. I expect the taxonomy to have like, you know, roughly 8 to 12 labels maybe, total. And then also put a rating for, like, they normally have to describe what they're currently doing with ChatGPT Work, use that to put a rating in the fifth column on how sophisticated their workflow already is. We're gonna be selecting a cohort of different people of different levels of sophistication, so we're gonna be like taking, you know, maybe like a couple dozens from like, a high level of sophistication, we're gonna take in the middle of a cohort, and then like low level of sophistication, so we have good representation and can test new features and get good feedback from a diverse group at different levels of adoption. So create that spreadsheet, go through all my DMs. Good luck!
remarks.translation: 我刚刚在 ChatGPT Work 里口述的内容 > 好的，我收到了很多私信，因为我之前发了个征集帖，想为 ChatGPT Work 组建一个早期体验测试群。我需要你把我 Twitter 上所有的私信都过一遍，把所有提到 ChatGPT Work 的私信挑出来。数量很多，大概有几千条，然后把它们全部整理到一个电子表格里。表格里我需要这几项：姓名、私信的链接（这样我能快速回看）、他们发的帖子内容（但要删掉所有换行符，让内容只放在一个单元格里）。然后还需要一列，是你对他们所做工作的评估，明白吗？比如，如果是销售类，就标“销售”；如果是营销类，就标“营销”；如果是设计类，就标“设计”。你自己定一套分类体系来划分他们的工作。我希望这套分类体系大概有 8 到 12 个标签，总共就这么多。另外，再给一个评分，他们通常得描述自己目前在用 ChatGPT Work 做什么，根据这个描述，在第五列里评一下他们现有工作流程的成熟度。我们会从不同成熟度的人群里选一批人，比如从高成熟度里挑几十个，中等成熟度里挑一批，低成熟度里也挑一批，这样就能有很好的代表性，可以测试新功能，并从不同采用程度的多元化群体那里获得有价值的反馈。所以，把这个表格做出来，把我所有的私信都过一遍。祝你好运！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
remarks.content: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good.</
remarks.contentTranslation: 在《用 Rust 重写 Bun》一文中，Jarred Sumner 提出了以下观点：Claude Code v2.1.181（于6月17日发布）及后续版本使用了 Bun 的 Rust 移植版。启动速度在 Linux 上提升了10%，但除此之外几乎无人察觉。平淡无奇，反而是好事。
remarks.translation: Claude Code 现在使用 Rust 编写的 Bun。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
Everyone should develop their "personal eval set" for AI models: a few tasks that are actually relevant to your day-to-day work/life

The industry benchmarks help but they might not reflect what will make it actually useful to you

You find the model's capability boundary by poking at it & bumping into it for fun
remarks.translation: 每个人都应该为AI模型建立自己的“个人评估集”：一些与你日常工作/生活真正相关的任务。行业基准固然有帮助，但它们未必能反映模型对你实际有用的程度。通过反复试探和碰撞，你才能找到模型的能力边界——这本身也是一种乐趣。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Based on internal evals:

▪️ Kimi K3 is top-tier at cybersecurity
There is chatter on X that Moonshot benchmark-overfit. These are stealth evals. Model has raw IQ.

▪️ Sol is a leap ahead in cyber capability
At a significantly higher cost, but quite remarkable still.

▪️ Fable refuses everything
We couldn’t get it to complete the run at all. What’s interesting is that Sol in comparison was much more open to helping with defensive cyber hardening

TL;DR: frontier, open-weight cybersecurity capability is here. Try it on https://t.co/HedSpZpo1a for defensive purposes.
remarks.translation: 基于内部评估：▪️ Kimi K3 在网络安全领域处于顶尖水平。X平台上有传言称Moonshot存在基准测试过拟合问题。这些是隐蔽评估。该模型具备原始智商。▪️ Sol在网络能力上实现跨越式进步。虽然成本显著更高，但依然相当出色。▪️ Fable拒绝一切操作。我们完全无法让它完成运行。有趣的是，相比之下Sol在协助防御性网络安全加固方面要开放得多。总结：前沿、开放权重的网络安全能力已经到来。请访问 https://t.co/HedSpZpo1a 尝试其防御用途。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.text:
My 8-year-old and I built a @ChatGPTapp Site to help her learn the multiplication tables.

She loves animals, so we:

→ Used ChatGPT Images to generate the UI and characters
→ Added some awesome music
→ Even added a timed boss level

📌 Play it here: https://t.co/sN7YBohW5R https://t.co/jEzmQjgBUe https://t.co/ghLmCVRmkk
remarks.translation: 我和8岁的女儿一起用@ChatGPTapp搭建了一个网站，帮她学习乘法表。她特别喜欢动物，所以我们：→ 用ChatGPT图像生成了界面和角色→ 添加了超棒的音乐→ 甚至还加入了限时Boss关卡📌 点此试玩：https://t.co/sN7YBohW5R https://t.co/jEzmQjgBUe https://t.co/ghLmCVRmkk

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
If you thought the value of the AI ecosystem was going to only accrue to a few companies, the past few months have represented a turning point in what the future of AI might look like. 

It's clear that there's going to be incredible innovation and growth coming out of the frontier AI labs, and they will continue to push the limits of what model progress looks like. They have the scale of compute, large revenue streams and customer bases, incredible researchers, large data pipelines, and more, to continue to stay at the forefront.

And at the same time, there’s an amazing ecosystem starting to play out to diffuse AI into the real world, taking on a variety of approaches that build on top of these frontier models or offer alternative visions that can credibly work as well. 

Here are just a few of the categories that seem to be working right now: 

* There’s an ecosystem of companies that will help enterprises and applied AI companies develop their own models tuned for specific use cases, and run the inference for them. This can drive additional performance gains and cost effective approaches to getting AI into workloads across different domains. 

* Applied AI companies that are delivering the end-user experience and business process tools necessary to actually driving enterprise adoption - including legal, IT, security, HR, customer support, coding, and more. These companies can work with any model and act as a routing layer, and deeply understand the enterprise workflow, can drive change management, actually get to the data necessary to work with, and more. 

* New labs are emerging that go deep in particular domains that are either not the focus areas of frontier labs or require a deep level of vertical expertise to stay ahead. Life sciences, financial services, healthcare, and more all have labs that will be able to bring completely new approaches to large enterprises across the economy.

* There’s all new infrastructure emerging to run models and agents effectively, protect and govern how they operate, store and secure the data they work with, and help enterprises orchestrate their activities. Multiple layers of this stack all are being built up right now that will help drive enterprise adoption.  

* New services firms that can actually drive the change management in enterprises necessary to the diffusion of AI. There will be hundreds or even thousands of new firms that emerge in lines of business or specific industries that can enable agents to be adopted.

I’m probably even missing a few categories, but this is what an incredible healthy technology ecosystem looks like. 

Way too early to call the winning architectures, and in reality it’s going to be a heterogenous environment as all other tech markets have become. Very exciting.
remarks.translation:
如果你曾以为AI生态的价值只会集中在少数几家公司身上，那么过去几个月无疑是一个转折点，预示着AI未来可能呈现的全新格局。显然，前沿AI实验室将带来惊人的创新与增长，并持续突破模型发展的边界。它们拥有庞大的算力规模、可观的收入流和客户基础、顶尖的研究人才、海量数据管道等优势，足以保持领先地位。与此同时，一个令人惊叹的生态系统正在形成，将AI渗透到现实世界，这些参与者或基于前沿模型构建多样化方案，或提供同样可信的替代路径。以下是目前看来正在奏效的几个类别：

* 一批公司正帮助企业及应用型AI公司开发针对特定场景调优的专属模型，并为其提供推理服务。这能带来额外的性能提升，并以高性价比的方式将AI融入不同领域的工作负载。
* 应用型AI公司正在提供终端用户体验和业务流程工具，真正推动企业采用AI——涵盖法律、IT、安全、人力资源、客户支持、编程等领域。这些公司可与任何模型协作，充当路由层，深度理解企业工作流程，推动变革管理，并切实获取所需数据。
* 新兴实验室正深耕特定领域，这些领域要么不是前沿实验室的重点方向，要么需要深厚的垂直专业知识才能保持领先。生命科学、金融服务、医疗健康等领域都涌现出实验室，它们将为各行业的大型企业带来全新解决方案。
* 全新的基础设施正在涌现，用于高效运行模型和智能体，保护并规范其运作方式，存储并保障其处理的数据安全，并帮助企业协调各项活动。这一技术栈的多个层面正在同步构建，将有力推动企业采用AI。
* 新型服务公司能够切实推动企业内部的变革管理，这对AI的普及至关重要。在业务线或特定行业中，将涌现数百甚至数千家新公司，助力智能体的落地应用。

我可能还遗漏了一些类别，但这正是一个极其健康的科技生态系统应有的模样。现在断言哪种架构会胜出为时过早，实际上，正如所有其他技术市场的发展历程所示，这将是一个多元异构的环境。令人无比振奋。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
The term "AGI" has aged very poorly. AI can't possibly me more different from human intelligence. It's *far better* than human intelligence, for most economically-relevant tasks.

But that doesn't make AIs better than humanity. There are wonderful things humans can do that these superintelligences (the better term) can't. 

For one, we care-for and care-about other humans in a powerful and inherent way, that we then teach machines to emulate. We must keep that as the top priority in everything we do. We have to be as pro-human-life as it gets.

AIs can replace tasks you do, but they can't replace the proverbial you. This is why they horribly suck at writing! Even when given a corpus of your writing and 200 Skills and 50 subagents running in gRaPh LoOps, they produce robotic prose… because they are robots. 

I know one definitive way that people can become irrelevant. They stop being themselves: lose their identity, delegate all their writing, their unique thoughts, the creative ways in which they can use the machines. Opting out of 'weighing in'.

I think we're safe though. There's nothing more distasteful right now than AI replies in social media or (🟢 T H I S    T H I N G ) in landing pages. Quality and humanity will prevail.
remarks.translation: “AGI”这个词已经过时得很厉害了。人工智能与人类智能的差异，简直不能再大了。在大多数与经济相关的任务上，它比人类智能*强得多*。但这并不意味着人工智能就比人类更优越。人类能做一些这些超级智能（更准确的说法）做不到的美妙事情。比如，我们以一种强大而内在的方式关心和关爱他人，然后我们才教会机器去模仿这一点。我们必须把这一点作为一切行动的最高优先。我们要尽可能地拥护人类生命。人工智能可以替代你做的任务，但它无法替代那个真正的你。这就是为什么它们在写作上糟糕透顶！即使给它们你的全部作品、200项技能和50个在图形循环中运行的子代理，它们写出的文字依然机械生硬……因为它们就是机器。我知道一个让人变得无足轻重的确切方式。那就是不再做自己：失去自我认同，把所有写作、独特想法、以及运用机器的创造性方式都交给别人。选择不再“发声”。不过，我觉得我们还算安全。现在没有什么比社交媒体上的人工智能回复，或者落地页上那个（🟢 这 玩 意 儿）更让人反感的了。品质和人性终将胜出。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

Google

remarks.content: TIL: Using uvx in GitHub Actions in a cache-friendly way I finally found a cache-friendly recipe for using uvx tool-name in GitHub Actions workflows that I like. <p

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 打造一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈相信它们确实成立——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个开放猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到它只是找到了一个反例时，他松了口气——这为他争取了又一两年时间，之后他毕生投入的事业就会变成 AI 更擅长的事情。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估的公司创始人和首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾认为是人类独有任务的速度有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
